Kate Garraway is 'smiling again' after growing close to fellow broadcaster Liam Halligan, two years after her husband tragically died. The Good Morning Britain host has formed a close friendship with Mr Halligan, who she has known for 20 years, the Daily Mail can reveal. He was also a friend of Ms Garraway's late spouse, Derek Draper. At the weekend, the television presenter joined Mr Halligan in his home town of Saffron Walden in Essex. She was seen 'getting cosy' with him at The Railway Arms pub - where he was performing with his band, The Hooligans.

The worse pain I’ve ever endured was from sciatic pain. Both sides, from back to big toe tip, numbness in one foot and finally changes to my bladder function. Top tip: if after 8 weeks there’s no improvements, push for an MRI and get a neurosurgeon to review the scan. To quote my neurosurgeon “no amount of conservative treatment, exercises or lifestyle adjustments are going to fix a massively ruptured disc, which has now fused around the nerve root. Part of the disc has split away completely, you have signs of Cauda Equina syndrome, and you’ll be in surgery in the next 12-24hrs.” G.P’s really need to explain the changes that people can experience from nerve compression, when they ask about bladder or bowel changes. Most people think it’s a loss of control but it can also be complete retention and the inability to go at all. To put the pain in context, it made child birth feel like a relaxing experience. It’s god awful, and morphine did n

Two brothers used a 'high level of violence' as they attacked armed police officer in Manchester Airport brawl, court hears

Two brothers attacked an armed police officer in an airport brawl using a 'high level of violence', a court heard on Monday. Mohammed Fahir Amaaz, 21, and his elder brother Muhammad Amaad, 26, rained 'repeated blows' on PC Zachary Marsden as he tried to arrest Amaaz in the parking payment area at Terminal Two in Manchester airport. Amaaz also acted 'in a cowardly manner' when he struck unarmed officer PC Lydia Ward by punching her in the face, breaking her nose and also hit PC Ellie Cook, an armed officer with 'a flurry of blows'.

EXCLUSIVETough love is not the vibe I would offer children of divorced parents, no matter their age. The break-up of a family is usually devastating and the children have no control in situations like these. Telling them that they can overcome adversity is one thing - telling them during this time of turmoil that "things will change as nothing is set in stone" is the most destabilizing mantra I can possibly think of. Your love for them doesn't change. His love for them doesn't change. The fact that they'll both always be there for their children won't change. That's what you tell them.

New food standards for schools in England will introduce a ban on deep fried foods from September next year - in an attempt to curb the UK's childhood obesity crisis. The move is set to take pupils away from the lunch canteens of the 60s, 70s, and 80s, which featured fish fingers and smiley face potatoes, spam fritters and cowboy pie. And for dessert, students could enjoy everything from a Jam Roly Poly - lathered in custard - to a frosty Arctic Roll.

Im sure the guy knew exactly how to do a flyover perfectly, need to chill and enjoy

A passenger jet pilot sparked fury in Iceland after flying a Boeing 757 at an 'alarmingly low' altitude above his hometown to celebrate his retirement. The pilot had been carrying out his final commercial flight after 40 years in service over Vestmannaeyjar, a volcanic archipelago off southern Iceland, on Saturday. Residents of the small island and passengers on board the flight from Frankfurt to Iceland were left startled when the pilot descended to just 100m over the community before landing at Keflavik International Airport.
  • 183 comments
  • 1 video

Lets not forget what all the scientist said about this, oh wait ,, none of it happened !!!!! Never mind !!!!

The world's largest iceberg has finally ended its ocean odyssey, crumbling into chunks just shy of its 40th birthday. A-23A is a 'megaberg' that weighed nearly a trillion tonnes and measured 1,540 square miles (3,988 square kilometres) at its peak - twice the size of Greater London. It originally calved from Antarctica's Filchner Ice Shelf back in 1986, remaining grounded on the seabed before beginning its slow journey in 2020.
  • 19 comments
  • 1 video
  • share World's largest iceberg finally disintegrates into small chunks

  • As one of the so called " more mature fans" I was there at Anfield on Wednesday March 16th 1977, when we beat St Etienne in one of THE greatest ever matches staged at Anfield. 49 years since that night and I can still remember it like it was yesterday. St Etienne fans flooding into Liverpool city centre at lunch time in their thousands. Gates shut 90 minutes before kick off ! Keegan scored inside 72 seconds. Ray Clemence makes wonder saves to repel the brilliant Dominique Rocheteau. Dominique Bathenay equalises with a fantastic 51st minute thunderbolt equaliser. Ray Kennedy scores to give us hope. Less than six minutes left and Fairclough runs to the Kop end and buries the winner ! Complete, utter unforgettable mayhem and bedlam. The last five minutes is a wall of incredible sound. I was totally dazed on my four mile journey home ! Anything, just anything is possible at Anfield !
